chapter_summary: Jesus teaches about marriage and divorce. He blesses little children when the disciples try to turn them away. A rich young man asks about eternal life but walks away sad. Jesus warns about the danger of riches. He predicts His death a third time. James and John ask for the best seats — Jesus teaches that greatness means serving others. Blind Bartimaeus cries out and is healed by his faith.
main_lesson: Jesus welcomes the overlooked — children, blind beggars, even sinners — and calls all His followers to serve, not to seek greatness. The kingdom of God belongs to those who come like children.

memory_verse.kjv: Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Let the children come to Me — do not stop them! The kingdom of God belongs to people who trust Me the way children do.'

parent_guide: Mark 10 addresses the full sweep of human relationships and social structures — marriage (vv1-12), children (vv13-16), wealth (vv17-31), ambition (vv35-45), and disability (vv46-52). For children, the three anchor stories are the blessing of the children (vv13-16 — Jesus was indignant that the disciples turned them away; He took them in His arms), the rich young ruler (vv17-22 — v21 says 'Jesus beholding him loved him,' showing that love does not always mean telling people what they want to hear), and Bartimaeus (vv46-52 — a model of persistent faith). The servant leadership passage (vv35-45) and the phrase 'ransom for many' (v45) introduce the substitutionary atonement in a child-accessible way.
application_for_children: You are never too small or too unimportant for Jesus. He always has time for you. Come to Him just as you are — like a child — and trust Him completely. And remember: in Jesus' kingdom, the greatest people are the ones who serve others.

verses.kjv: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judaea by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus left that place and travelled to the region of Judea, on the other side of the Jordan River. Crowds came to Him again, and — just like always — He sat down and began to teach them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to a new place. Crowds came to Him and He taught them, just like always.
verses.meaning: Wherever Jesus went, people followed because He had what they needed. Teaching was always His first response — the Word of God was His greatest gift to give.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus always teaches those who come.
verses.prayer: Jesus, wherever I go today, help me remember that You are already there, ready to teach me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think people kept following Jesus everywhere He went?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus made teaching His habit — 'as he was wont' means He always did it. What habits does your family have that help you stay close to God?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible today, just to listen for something Jesus wants to teach you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking along a dusty road near a wide river, a colourful crowd of families streaming toward Him from all directions, rolling green hills in the background, soft morning light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
verses.little_bible: Some Pharisees came to Jesus with a tricky question — trying to catch Him out. They asked: 'Does God's law allow a man to divorce his wife?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some religious leaders tried to trick Jesus with a hard question about marriage.
verses.meaning: The Pharisees weren't looking for truth — they were trying to trap Jesus. But Jesus always turned tricky questions into moments of real teaching.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus cannot be tricked.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me come to You with honest questions, not to trick You but to learn from You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been asked a question that felt like a trick? How did you handle it?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think the religious leaders kept trying to catch Jesus? What does that tell us about what happens when we don't want to hear the truth?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God one honest question today — and then wait quietly to see if He speaks to your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of serious-looking men in religious robes approaching Jesus on a road, arms crossed, expressions challenging, while Jesus stands calm and steady. Afternoon light, watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
verses.little_bible: Instead of answering right away, Jesus turned the question back to them: 'What did Moses tell you in God's law?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked them, 'What did Moses teach you?' He answered their question with a question.
verses.meaning: Jesus was a wise teacher. He pointed them back to Scripture — God's own Word — instead of just giving His opinion. Truth comes from God, not from clever debate.
verses.memory_phrase: Go back to God's Word.
verses.prayer: God, when I have questions, help me look in Your Word first before I look anywhere else.
verses.discussion_question: When you have a big question, where is the first place you look for an answer?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus pointed the Pharisees back to the Scriptures. How does your family use the Bible to answer hard questions about life?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a question on your mind, look in the Bible together as a family for something that helps answer it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing with one hand raised gently, looking at the religious leaders with calm wisdom, sunlight behind Him, open landscape stretching out beyond. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.
verses.little_bible: They answered: 'Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and send his wife away.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said Moses allowed men to write a paper and send their wife away.
verses.meaning: The leaders quoted a real part of the law — but they were using it to justify doing something that hurt people, without understanding why the rule existed in the first place.
verses.memory_phrase: Rules have reasons — ask God why.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me understand not just what Your rules say, but why You made them — because You love us.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a rule at home or school that was made to protect someone? What is it?
verses.family_discussion: God's laws are always about love and protection. Talk about a time a rule felt hard to follow but turned out to be good for you.
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent or teacher to explain the reason behind one rule that seems hard. Listen carefully.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two religious leaders gesturing with scrolls in their hands, looking confident, while Jesus listens patiently. Stone pathway, warm light, people watching nearby. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'Moses gave you that rule because your hearts were hard and stubborn. It was not God's first plan — it was a concession because of sin.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said Moses made that rule because people's hearts were hard and stubborn.
verses.meaning: God sometimes allows things because of human stubbornness, even though they are not His perfect plan. His heart has always been for love, faithfulness, and wholeness.
verses.memory_phrase: God's plan is always love.
verses.prayer: God, soften my heart so I want what You want, not just what is easiest for me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have a 'hard heart'? Can you give an example?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said some rules existed because of people's hard hearts. How can we keep our hearts soft toward God and toward each other at home?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to make your heart soft and ready to obey Him today — then look for one chance to show love to someone at home.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking gently but firmly, His hand over His heart, the religious leaders looking uncertain, a soft golden light in the background. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'From the very beginning — when God first made everything — He made people male and female. That was always His design.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said that from the very beginning God made people male and female.
verses.meaning: Jesus went back to creation — to God's original design before sin entered the world. God thought carefully about people and relationships from the very start.
verses.memory_phrase: God made us on purpose.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for making me exactly the way You did. You planned me from the beginning.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that God made us 'from the beginning'? Does that make you feel important?
verses.family_discussion: God's design for people was not an accident — it was a plan made with love. How does knowing God designed your family make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for making you. Draw or write one thing you love about how God made you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautiful garden scene glowing with warm sunrise light, two figures standing together in lush greenery, the world fresh and new around them. Gentle, peaceful watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'This is why a man leaves his parents and holds tightly to his wife — God designed marriage as a deep and special bond.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said a man should leave his parents and hold tightly to his wife.
verses.meaning: Marriage was designed by God as the deepest human commitment — two people choosing each other above all others, held together by God's love.
verses.memory_phrase: God designed marriage as a gift.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the gift of family. Help people in my family to love each other well.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think leaving your parents and starting a family is such a big, important thing?
verses.family_discussion: God put marriage and family at the centre of His design for people. How does your family show love and stick together even when things are hard?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for every person in your family today — one small act of love for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young couple standing together on a sunlit hillside, hands clasped, looking out over a beautiful landscape, family in the background blessing them. Soft watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The two people become one. They are no longer two separate people going their own way — God joins them together as one.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said a husband and wife become one — God joins them together.
verses.meaning: God's design for marriage is unity — two lives woven together so tightly that they become one. This is a picture of how deeply God loves His people too.
verses.memory_phrase: God joins people together.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for loving us so much that You want to be close to us. Help families stay close to each other and to You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'becoming one' means for a family? How does your family work together?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible says God joins a husband and wife together. Talk about ways your family acts as a team — and how God is part of that team.
verses.do_it_today: Do something as a family team today — cook a meal together, clean a room, or pray together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two hands intertwined, surrounded by soft golden light and gentle green leaves, a sense of warmth and unity radiating from the image. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'What God has joined together, no person should pull apart. When God ties two lives together, it is meant to last.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said what God joins together, people should not tear apart.
verses.meaning: God takes marriage seriously because He is the one who designed it. Breaking apart what He has joined always causes pain — to people and to His heart.
verses.memory_phrase: What God joins, let it stay.
verses.prayer: God, help me value what You have made and never take for granted the people You have placed in my life.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think it hurts when families are broken apart? How can we help families stay together?
verses.family_discussion: God's heart is for families to be whole and together. Pray together for any families you know who are hurting or broken apart.
verses.do_it_today: Write a prayer for one family you know that needs God's help to stay together or be healed.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two rivers flowing together and becoming one wide, peaceful river, light glinting on the water, green banks on either side, a quiet and beautiful scene. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
verses.little_bible: Later, when they were inside a house, the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked Him more questions about what He had just taught.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Later, the disciples asked Jesus more questions about this when they were inside.
verses.meaning: The disciples wanted to understand more deeply. Private conversations with Jesus were just as important as public ones — He always had more to teach.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask Jesus your deeper questions.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to understand You better. Help me ask good questions and really listen for Your answers.
verses.discussion_question: Is there something you have heard about God that you want to understand better? What is it?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples asked Jesus questions in private. Does your family have a time and place to ask God honest questions together?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one question you have for God. Bring it to a parent, Sunday school teacher, or in your prayer tonight.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small warm room with oil lamps glowing, disciples sitting close around Jesus on the floor, leaning in with curious and thoughtful expressions, evening light through a small window. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told His disciples: 'If a man divorces his wife and marries someone else, he has broken the promise he made to her — that is sin.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said promises in marriage must be kept. Breaking them is a serious sin.
verses.meaning: Jesus held marriage to a high standard because He cares about people — especially those who could be hurt when promises are broken. Faithfulness is the heart of love.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep your promises — they matter to God.
verses.prayer: God, help me be someone who keeps promises. Make me faithful in all my relationships.
verses.discussion_question: Why is keeping a promise so important? How do you feel when someone breaks a promise to you?
verses.family_discussion: God takes promises very seriously. Talk about the promises your family makes to each other and how you can help each other keep them.
verses.do_it_today: Think of a promise you have made. Do something today to keep it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking seriously and compassionately to His disciples gathered closely around Him in a small room, one lamp casting warm light, the disciples' faces reflecting deep thought. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
verses.little_bible: Jesus added: 'And if a woman leaves her husband and marries someone else, that is also sin.' God's standard of faithfulness applies to everyone equally.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said this applies to women too — God's rules about faithfulness are for everyone.
verses.meaning: Jesus affirmed that women and men are equally accountable to God — a revolutionary statement in that culture. God's love and His standards apply to all people equally.
verses.memory_phrase: God's love and truth are for everyone.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You treat every person as important and valuable. Help me treat others the same way.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important that God's rules are fair for everyone — boys and girls, men and women?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus showed that God values all people equally. How can your family make sure everyone feels equally loved and important at home?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind and fair for someone you might sometimes overlook or forget about today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm family scene, a woman and man facing each other with gentle expressions, surrounded by soft golden light, children nearby, a home in the background. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
verses.little_bible: People started bringing their little children to Jesus so He could touch them and bless them. But the disciples told them to stop and go away — they thought children were bothering Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Parents brought children to Jesus. The disciples told them to go away and stop bothering Jesus.
verses.meaning: The disciples thought Jesus was too important for small children. They were completely wrong. This moment shows how easily even good people can misunderstand what God truly values.
verses.memory_phrase: No one is too small for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You never turn me away. I know I can always come to You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like you were too small or unimportant for something? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples thought children weren't important enough for Jesus' time. What does that show about how we can misjudge people? Who might we be leaving out?
verses.do_it_today: Include someone younger or smaller than you in what you are doing today. Let them know they matter.
verses.illustration_prompt: Parents carrying babies and holding toddlers by the hand, walking eagerly toward Jesus in the distance, while several disciples stand with arms outstretched blocking the way, looking stern. Warm outdoor light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus saw what was happening, He was very upset with His disciples. He said: 'Let the little children come to Me — do not stop them! The kingdom of God belongs to people who trust Me the way children do.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was upset and said: 'Let the children come! Don't stop them. The kingdom of God belongs to people like them.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was indignant — genuinely angry — that children were being kept from Him. This is the heart of Jesus: He always has time for the small, the overlooked, and the unimportant. YOU are never too small for Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus says: 'Let the children come!'
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You want me to come to You. I come to You right now, just as I am.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was upset when children were turned away. What does that tell you about how much He loves you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the kingdom belongs to those who come like children — with trust, openness, and no pretending. How can your family come to God more like a child this week?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus in prayer right now, just like a little child — honest, simple, and trusting. Tell Him something you need.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling on the ground with children climbing into His arms and sitting in His lap, His face full of joy and warmth, disciples standing nearby looking surprised and humbled, warm golden afternoon light flooding the scene. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I am telling you the truth — unless you receive God's kingdom the way a little child does — with trust and an open heart — you will never get in.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You must receive God's kingdom like a little child — with total trust.' That is the only way in.
verses.meaning: Children trust completely, ask freely, and don't pretend to be better than they are. That is exactly how God wants us to come to Him. Pride and self-sufficiency lock the door; childlike faith opens it.
verses.memory_phrase: Trust God like a child.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust You completely — like a small child trusts a good parent. I don't want to pretend to be strong. I just want to trust You.
verses.discussion_question: What does a child do when they are scared or hungry? How can we do the same with God?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said childlike faith is the key to the kingdom. What does childlike trust look like in your family's relationship with God? Is there an area where you need to trust Him more simply?
verses.do_it_today: Practice childlike prayer today — just talk to God simply, honestly, about exactly what you need and feel.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small child with arms wide open running toward Jesus, pure joy on their face, green meadow all around, Jesus crouching down with arms open to receive them, soft sunlight streaming behind. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus picked the children up in His arms, placed His hands gently on them, and blessed each one. He did not just wave at them — He held them close and prayed over them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus picked the children up in His arms, put His hands on them, and blessed every single one.
verses.meaning: Jesus didn't just approve of the children from a distance — He picked them up, held them, and blessed them personally. This is what Jesus is like: close, warm, and intentional with every person who comes to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus holds you close and blesses you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for holding me. I know You see me and You bless me. Help me feel how close You are.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to be hugged by someone who loves you? That is what Jesus did for those children.
verses.family_discussion: Jesus blessed each child personally — not in a group, but one by one. How do you bless each person in your family individually? Try blessing each other tonight before bed.
verses.do_it_today: Hug every person in your family today. Tell them: 'Jesus loves you and so do I.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling on the ground with children climbing into His arms and sitting in His lap, His face full of joy and warmth, one child touching His beard, another resting on His shoulder, disciples standing nearby looking surprised and humbled, warm golden afternoon light flooding the scene. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled before him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
verses.little_bible: As Jesus was leaving, a young man came running up to Him and fell on his knees. He asked: 'Good Teacher, what do I have to do to get eternal life?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A young man ran to Jesus, knelt down, and asked: 'What must I do to have eternal life?'
verses.meaning: This young man was eager and sincere — he ran to Jesus and knelt before Him. He asked the most important question any person can ask. But notice what he asked: 'What do I DO?' He was still thinking about earning God's gift.
verses.memory_phrase: The greatest question: 'How do I have eternal life?'
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that eternal life is a gift from You. Help me come to You with my whole heart, just like this young man did.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'eternal life' means? Why is it the most important question to ask?
verses.family_discussion: This young man asked the most important question of his life. Have you asked Jesus the same question? Talk about what eternal life means to each person in your family.
verses.do_it_today: Write down the most important question you have ever asked or want to ask God. Bring it to Him in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A well-dressed young man in fine clothes running along a dusty road, then dropping to his knees before Jesus with an earnest, hopeful expression, a crowd watching from behind. Warm afternoon light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied: 'Why do you call Me good? Only God is truly good.' Jesus was pointing the young man to think carefully — who is Jesus, really? And where does all goodness come from?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Why do you call Me good? Only God is truly good.' He wanted the young man to think carefully.
verses.meaning: Jesus was not denying He was good — He was God! He was inviting the young man to think: if Jesus is truly good, then Jesus is God. This was a gentle challenge to go deeper.
verses.memory_phrase: All goodness comes from God.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You are perfectly good. Help me see how good You are and trust You more because of it.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean for someone to be truly good? Can any person be perfectly good on their own?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said only God is truly good. What does that mean for how we live? How does knowing God is perfectly good help us trust Him?
verses.do_it_today: Look for one example of God's goodness today — in nature, in a kindness shown to you, or in something you read. Thank God for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking at the young man with gentle, searching eyes, one hand raised, an expression of patient wisdom on His face, the young man looking thoughtful and slightly unsure. Bright blue sky behind them. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
verses.little_bible: Jesus listed God's commandments: 'You know the rules — do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not lie about others, do not cheat anyone, and honour your parents.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus listed God's rules: don't lie, don't steal, don't hurt others, and honour your parents.
verses.meaning: Jesus started with the commandments the young man already knew — the ones about how we treat other people. This was not the end of the conversation; it was the beginning.
verses.memory_phrase: God's rules protect people we love.
verses.prayer: God, help me obey Your rules — not to earn love, but because I love You and I love people.
verses.discussion_question: Can you name any of God's commandments? Why do you think God gave us rules?
verses.family_discussion: God's commandments are about loving people well. Which commandment does your family find easiest to keep? Which one is hardest?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one of God's commandments and practice keeping it on purpose today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus counting commandments on His fingers, speaking kindly but directly to the kneeling young man, the crowd listening around them, a clear sky above. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.
verses.little_bible: The young man answered: 'Teacher, I have kept all of those rules since I was a boy.' He had tried very hard his whole life — but something was still missing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The young man said: 'I have kept all those rules since I was young.' He had tried his best — but something was missing.
verses.meaning: He was sincere and not boasting — he had genuinely tried hard to obey God's rules. But rule-keeping alone doesn't reach the deepest place in our hearts. Jesus was about to show him what was truly missing.
verses.memory_phrase: Obeying rules is not enough — we need Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me see that following rules is good, but what I really need is YOU. Come into my heart and change me from the inside.
verses.discussion_question: Is it possible to do all the right things and still feel like something is missing? What could that thing be?
verses.family_discussion: This young man did everything right by the rules — but still lacked something. Talk about the difference between following rules and having a real relationship with God.
verses.do_it_today: Instead of just trying to behave well today, talk to Jesus honestly. Ask Him to fill the empty places in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sincere young man looking at Jesus with hope and slight pride in his expression, his fine clothes neat and tidy, hands on his chest, the disciples watching from behind. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at him and loved him deeply. Then He said: 'There is one thing you are missing. Sell everything you own, give it to the poor — you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, pick up your cross, and follow Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus looked at him and loved him. Then He said: 'One thing is missing — sell all you have, give it to the poor, and follow Me.'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most tender verses in all the Gospels — 'Jesus beholding him loved him.' Jesus loved him AND told him the hard truth. True love always does that. The young man's money was his real master. Jesus invited him to trade temporary treasure for eternal treasure.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus loves you enough to tell the truth.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for loving me enough to tell me what I really need to hear. Help me trust You with everything I hold on to too tightly.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between loving someone and always telling them what they want to hear?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus loved the young man AND told him hard truth. Talk about a time someone loved you enough to tell you something you needed to hear, even when it was hard.
verses.do_it_today: Look for something you are holding onto too tightly — a toy, a plan, a worry. Give it to God in prayer today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling to look the young man in the eyes, His expression full of deep, warm love, one hand on the young man's shoulder, the young man's face showing a mixture of awe and inner struggle. Golden afternoon light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
verses.little_bible: The young man's face fell. He was very sad when he heard what Jesus said — and he walked away with a heavy heart. He had so much money and so many things that he couldn't let them go.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The young man's face fell. He walked away very sad because he had too much stuff to let go of.
verses.meaning: He chose his things over Jesus — and walked away. This is one of the saddest moments in the Gospels. Jesus let him go. Love never forces. But the door was still open; Jesus still loved him.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't let stuff keep you from Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me never love things more than I love You. You are worth more than anything I own.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you love so much that it would be very hard to give up? What would you say to Jesus about that?
verses.family_discussion: The young man walked away sad. Talk about what holds people back from following Jesus. Is there anything your family is holding onto that might be getting in the way?
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today — a toy, food, money, or time — to someone who needs it more than you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man in fine clothes walking away down a dusty road, head bowed, shoulders drooping, while Jesus watches him go with a gentle and sorrowful expression. Long shadows, late afternoon light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at His disciples and said: 'How hard it is for people with great riches to enter the kingdom of God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said it is very hard for rich people to enter God's kingdom.
verses.meaning: Money is not evil — but trusting in money instead of God is. The more we have, the harder it can be to feel our need for God. Jesus was warning His disciples about a real and dangerous trap.
verses.memory_phrase: Trust God, not your stuff.
verses.prayer: God, help me never trust things more than I trust You. You are my real treasure and my real security.
verses.discussion_question: Why might having lots of things make it harder to trust God?
verses.family_discussion: Riches can make us feel like we don't need God. Talk about things your family might be tempted to depend on instead of God — and how to keep God first.
verses.do_it_today: Make a list of five things you are grateful for. Thank God for each one — and tell Him He is better than all of them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to His disciples with a serious and caring expression, gesturing back toward the road where the young man has just walked away, disciples looking thoughtful and surprised. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
verses.little_bible: The disciples were shocked by what He said. Jesus continued — even calling them 'children' — 'How hard it is for those who TRUST IN riches to enter the kingdom!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples were shocked. Jesus said again: 'It is so hard for those who trust in riches!'
verses.meaning: Notice that Jesus called His disciples 'children' here — with gentleness and affection. He repeated the warning because it was important. The danger is not having money — it is trusting it.
verses.memory_phrase: Trust in God, not in money.
verses.prayer: Lord, teach me that You are enough. Help me put all my trust in You, not in what I have.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'trust' in something? What do you really trust to keep you safe?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus used the word 'trust' — the real danger is trusting money to provide what only God can give. What are you trusting God for in your family right now?
verses.do_it_today: Make a 'trust list' — write three things you need to trust God for instead of trying to handle alone.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a gentle smile speaking to His amazed disciples who are clustered around Him on a hillside path, some with mouths open, one scratching his head. Soft blue-green hills in background. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'It is easier to push a camel through the tiny hole of a sewing needle than for a person who trusts in riches to enter God's kingdom!' It is a funny picture — but it makes the point perfectly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's tiny hole than for a rich man to enter God's kingdom!
verses.meaning: Jesus used an exaggerated, funny image — a huge camel trying to squeeze through a tiny needle's hole — to make the point impossible to forget. Trusting in riches makes it nearly impossible to depend on God.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom can't be bought.
verses.prayer: God, help me never think I can earn or buy my way into Your kingdom. I come to You empty-handed and grateful.
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine a camel going through a needle hole? Why do you think Jesus used such a funny example?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told a story so silly it was unforgettable. What is the most important truth your family has learned about money and God?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of a camel trying to go through a needle hole — then write underneath: 'God's kingdom is free, not for sale.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A comically huge camel squinting at a tiny glinting needle held by a hand, with warm colours and a whimsical storybook feel, desert sand and blue sky in the background. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?
verses.little_bible: The disciples were completely amazed. They whispered to each other: 'If that's true, who can possibly be saved?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples were amazed and worried. They asked each other: 'Then who can be saved?'
verses.meaning: In that culture, people believed wealth was a sign of God's blessing. If even the wealthy couldn't be saved, then salvation seemed impossible for anyone. But that's exactly where Jesus wanted them — at the end of themselves, ready for God's answer.
verses.memory_phrase: When we are desperate, God answers.
verses.prayer: God, I know I cannot save myself. I depend completely on You. Thank You for making a way.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like something was impossible — and then someone helped you? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples arrived at the right question — 'Who can be saved?' — and now they were ready for the right answer. Talk about times your family has had to admit they needed God's help.
verses.do_it_today: Write or say this prayer: 'God, I know I cannot save myself. I need You. Thank You for saving me through Jesus.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples huddled together on a hillside path, faces full of wonder and concern, whispering to each other with wide eyes, Jesus standing nearby watching them with a calm expression. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at them and said: 'By human effort alone, it is impossible. But not with God! With God, ALL things are possible.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'With people it is impossible. But with God, all things are possible!'
verses.meaning: This is one of the greatest promises in the Bible. Salvation is not something we achieve — it is something God does. What is impossible for us is perfectly possible for Him.
verses.memory_phrase: With God, all things are possible.
verses.prayer: God, I believe You can do what I cannot. Nothing is too hard for You. Help me trust in Your power, not my own.
verses.discussion_question: What is something that seems impossible? Can you trust God with it?
verses.family_discussion: This verse is a lifeline. Talk about something in your family that seems impossible right now — and pray over it together, believing God can do what you cannot.
verses.do_it_today: Write 'With God all things are possible' somewhere you will see it every day this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking up with a confident smile, one hand raised toward the sky, the disciples around Him slowly shifting from worry to hope, warm golden light breaking through clouds above. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
verses.little_bible: Peter spoke up: 'Look — we have left everything behind to follow You. We gave up our jobs, our homes, our old lives. What about us?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter said: 'We left everything to follow You, Jesus. What will happen to us?'
verses.meaning: Peter was honest — and a little worried. He and the others had made real sacrifices. Jesus was about to show them that following Him always leads to MORE, never to less.
verses.memory_phrase: When you give up something for Jesus, He gives back more.
verses.prayer: Jesus, whatever I give up to follow You, I trust that You are worth it. Help me follow You without looking back.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever given something up to do the right thing? How did it turn out?
verses.family_discussion: Peter was honest about what following Jesus cost them. Is following Jesus costing your family anything right now? How has God been faithful in return?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing you have given up or could give up to follow Jesus more closely. Thank Him for being worth it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter with a hand on his chest speaking earnestly to Jesus, the other disciples nodding behind him, a road stretching away behind them with boats and fishing nets visible in the distance. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel's,
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'I am telling you the truth — no one who has left their home, their brothers or sisters, their father or mother, wife, children, or land for My sake and for the good news of God —'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Anyone who has left family or home for My sake and the gospel's —'
verses.meaning: Jesus takes every sacrifice seriously. He lists everything a person might give up — family, home, land — and says He notices every single one. Nothing given for His sake goes unnoticed.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees every sacrifice made for Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You see every hard thing I do for You. Nothing I give up for You is ever wasted.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of someone who gave up something big to serve God? What did they give up?
verses.family_discussion: Talk about missionaries, pastors, or people in your community who have given up comfort for the sake of the gospel. How can you encourage them?
verses.do_it_today: Write a note or pray for someone you know who has made a sacrifice to serve God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus counting on His fingers with a gentle and knowing expression, the disciples listening intently around Him, a road stretching behind them, warm early evening light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: '— that person will receive a hundred times as much in this life: more family, more home, more belonging than they ever had before — though with some hard times too — and in eternity, life forever with God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said they will receive a hundred times more — a huge family in God, and forever life with Him!
verses.meaning: Following Jesus gives us an enormous family — the whole church — and ultimately eternal life. Jesus is honest that there will be hard times ('with persecutions') but the eternal reward is beyond comparison.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus gives back more than we give up.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that following You is always worth it. Help me keep my eyes on the eternal reward, not just the cost.
verses.discussion_question: Have you discovered a new 'family' through church or through your friends who love Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus promised that giving up for Him brings an even bigger family — the church. How have you experienced the church as family? How can you give more to that family?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone in your church family this week — visit, call, or make something for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful scene of a large diverse crowd of people gathered around Jesus, laughing and embracing like family, with homes and green land in the background, a warm festive feeling, sunshine. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'Many who seem like the most important people will be the least important in God's kingdom — and many who seem least important will be the greatest.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.' God sees differently than we do.
verses.meaning: God's kingdom upends the world's ranking system. The humble, the overlooked, the ones who serve — they are the great ones in God's eyes. This is the recurring theme of all of Mark 10.
verses.memory_phrase: In God's kingdom, the last goes first.
verses.prayer: God, help me stop trying to be first. Help me serve others and trust You to take care of my place in Your kingdom.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of someone who seems unimportant to the world but who might be very great in God's eyes?
verses.family_discussion: God's value system is upside down from the world's. Who are the 'last' people in your community — and how might you honour them the way God does?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind today for someone who is often forgotten or left out.
verses.illustration_prompt: A winding line of very different people — some in fine clothes, some in ragged ones — with the people in simple clothes leading and the ones in fine clothes at the back, set against a glowing kingdom skyline. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,
verses.little_bible: They were on the road going up to Jerusalem. Jesus walked ahead of them — and the disciples were amazed but also afraid. Then Jesus pulled His twelve closest disciples aside and began to tell them what was about to happen to Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus walked ahead toward Jerusalem. The disciples were amazed and afraid. Jesus told them what was going to happen to Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus walked toward Jerusalem with total courage and purpose. He knew what was coming — betrayal, suffering, death — and He walked toward it anyway. He walked ahead of His disciples, leading the way.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walked ahead, leading the way.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You go ahead of me into every hard place. Help me follow You even when I am afraid.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had to walk toward something scary? Did you have someone walk with you or ahead of you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus walked toward suffering on purpose — for us. How does knowing He went first help you face your own hard times?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something that scares you. Imagine Jesus walking ahead of you into it. Pray and ask Him to lead the way.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus striding purposefully ahead on a long winding road toward a city on a hill, the twelve disciples following at a slight distance, some looking worried, some amazed, late afternoon sky above. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'We are going to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man — Me — will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. They will decide I must die and hand Me over to the Romans.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He would be handed over to the religious leaders who would condemn Him to die and give Him to the Romans.
verses.meaning: Jesus gave His disciples a detailed prediction of exactly what would happen — not to frighten them, but to prepare them, and to show that nothing was happening by accident. He was in control.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew what was coming and still came.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for choosing to go through all of that for me. Help me understand what Your love truly cost You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus knew exactly what was going to happen to Him. Why do you think He still kept going?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus walked toward betrayal and death — because He loved us. Talk about what it means that Jesus knew all this and still went to Jerusalem for us.
verses.do_it_today: Read John 15:13 together: 'Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one's life for one's friends.' Thank Jesus for doing that for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hillside road speaking solemnly to His twelve disciples who are gathered closely around Him, Jerusalem visible in the distance, late golden light, serious and reverent atmosphere. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'They will make fun of Me, beat Me, and spit on Me — and they will kill Me. But on the third day, I will rise back to life!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He would be hurt and killed. But He also said: 'On the third day, I will rise again!'
verses.meaning: Jesus described the worst and the best in the same breath — suffering and resurrection. He wanted His disciples to hold both: yes, things will be very dark. But the story does not end there.
verses.memory_phrase: He will rise again — death is not the end!
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for dying for me and for rising again. Because You are alive, I will live too! Help me never forget that.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus told His disciples the hard part AND the wonderful part. Why is the resurrection so important?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told His disciples they would see Him suffer — but also that He would rise. How does the resurrection change how we face hard things in our own lives?
verses.do_it_today: Say together out loud: 'Jesus died. Jesus rose. Jesus is alive today!' Celebrate that truth.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking to His disciples with one hand raised, a shaft of sunlight breaking through clouds above, the disciples' faces mixed with sorrow and dawning hope, the sky behind them moving from dark to bright. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we desire.
verses.little_bible: Then James and John — the sons of Zebedee — came to Jesus with a request. They said: 'Teacher, we want You to do whatever we ask!' They were about to ask for something very big.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: James and John went to Jesus and said: 'We want You to do whatever we ask!' They had a big selfish request.
verses.meaning: After Jesus had just told them about His coming suffering, James and John were thinking about their own promotion. It is uncomfortable — but also very human. Jesus responded with patience.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't seek greatness — seek to serve.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me stop trying to get the best seat for myself. Help me think about how I can serve others instead.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wanted to be the most important person in a group? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: James and John asked for the best positions. Talk about the difference between wanting to be great and wanting to serve. Which one does your family want to be known for?
verses.do_it_today: Find one way to serve someone else today — without expecting any reward or recognition.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two young men approaching Jesus with eager, slightly sheepish expressions, leaning in close as though making a secret request, the other disciples nearby not yet hearing. Warm light, outdoor setting. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them simply: 'What do you want Me to do for you?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'What do you want Me to do for you?'
verses.meaning: Jesus asked the same question He would later ask blind Bartimaeus. He always wants us to say what we need honestly. He listened before He answered — even when the request was wrong.
verses.memory_phrase: Tell Jesus honestly what you need.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for always asking what I need and for listening. Help me be honest with You.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus asked you right now, 'What do you want Me to do for you?' — what would you say?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave James and John the chance to say what was in their hearts. Does your family talk openly to God about what you really want? How can you be more honest in prayer?
verses.do_it_today: In your prayers tonight, answer this question honestly: 'Jesus, what I really want You to do for me is...'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus facing James and John with a calm, attentive expression and listening posture, one eyebrow raised gently, the two disciples looking slightly nervous. Blue sky and open hillside behind. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
verses.little_bible: They said: 'We want to sit beside You — one on Your right, one on Your left — when You come into Your glory and Your kingdom!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: James and John said: 'Let us sit beside You — one on each side — when You are King!'
verses.meaning: They wanted the highest seats of honour in Jesus' kingdom. The irony is that the two people who would sit on Jesus' left and right at the crucifixion were criminals. Glory and suffering were more connected than they knew.
verses.memory_phrase: Glory in God's kingdom looks like service.
verses.prayer: God, help me understand that true glory in Your kingdom looks like giving — not grabbing. Shape my heart to want what You want.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'glory' means? What does glory look like in God's kingdom?
verses.family_discussion: James and John thought glory meant sitting in the best seat. Jesus showed that glory meant the cross. Talk about how Jesus changed what it means to be great.
verses.do_it_today: Instead of trying to get the best spot today, deliberately take the less important one and see how it feels.
verses.illustration_prompt: James and John standing confidently before Jesus, gesturing and pointing to positions on either side of Him, looking pleased with their request, Jesus listening with a thoughtful expression. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You don't understand what you are asking for. Can you drink from the painful cup that I am about to drink? Can you go through what I am about to go through?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You don't know what you're asking. Can you go through what I am about to go through?'
verses.meaning: The 'cup' Jesus referred to was His coming suffering and death. Real greatness in God's kingdom is not about privilege — it is about sacrifice. Jesus wanted them to understand what true glory costs.
verses.memory_phrase: Greatness costs something — but Jesus paid the most.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me understand how much You suffered for me. Help me not ask for glory but be willing to serve and give.
verses.discussion_question: What is a 'cup of suffering'? Can you think of something hard someone went through to help others?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus challenged James and John to think about what real greatness costs. Talk about what it might cost your family to truly follow Jesus — and whether it is worth it.
verses.do_it_today: Read Isaiah 53:3-5 together. Thank Jesus for drinking the cup of suffering so you don't have to.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding up an imaginary cup with a serious and searching expression, looking into the eyes of James and John who are starting to look uncertain, the disciples gathered around. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
verses.little_bible: They said confidently: 'We can do it!' Jesus replied: 'Yes — you will indeed share in what I go through. You will face suffering for My sake.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: James and John said: 'We can!' Jesus said: 'Yes, you will suffer for Me too.'
verses.meaning: Jesus confirmed that they would suffer — and they did. Both faced persecution. James was the first apostle martyred. Jesus' words were not a punishment but a promise of partnership in His mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Sharing in Jesus' suffering is an honour.
verses.prayer: God, if I face hard things because I follow Jesus, help me remember it is an honour to share in His story.
verses.discussion_question: Is it scary to think about facing hard things for following Jesus? What might help you be brave?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus warned that following Him means sharing in His suffering. Talk about Christians around the world who face real suffering for their faith. Pray for them together.
verses.do_it_today: Look up one country where Christians face persecution. Pray for them by name.
verses.illustration_prompt: James and John looking determined and brave, chests forward, Jesus nodding slowly and seriously, the weight of the moment in the atmosphere, other disciples listening quietly. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But sitting at My right and My left in the kingdom — that is not Mine to give. Those places belong to the people God the Father has prepared them for.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said those special seats were not His to give — God the Father has prepared them for certain people.
verses.meaning: Jesus was completely honest. He did not promise what He could not give. The positions of ultimate honour in God's kingdom belong to those God has specially prepared — a reminder that God's plans are beyond our calculations.
verses.memory_phrase: God prepares special places for His people.
verses.prayer: God, I trust You to prepare the right place for me. I don't need to grab — I just need to trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Does it feel okay that God decides who gets which place in His kingdom? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was honest that some things are in God the Father's hands alone. Are there things in your family's life that you need to trust to God's hands instead of trying to control them?
verses.do_it_today: Let go of one thing you've been trying to control. Say out loud: 'God, I trust this to You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with both hands open and upward in a gesture of honest surrender, looking gently at James and John, a soft bright sky behind Him, an atmosphere of peace and trust. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And when the ten heard it, they began to be much displeased with James and John.
verses.little_bible: When the other ten disciples heard what James and John had asked, they were very upset and angry with them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The other ten disciples heard what James and John asked, and they were angry at them.
verses.meaning: The other disciples were angry — but probably not because James and John were wrong to want greatness. They were upset because James and John had beaten them to the same request. Pride and comparison can poison community.
verses.memory_phrase: Pride divides; humility unites.
verses.prayer: God, keep pride and jealousy out of my heart. Help me be glad when others do well.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been upset because someone else got something you wanted? How did you handle it?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples fell into comparison and competition. How does your family handle jealousy when it comes up? How can you encourage each other instead of competing?
verses.do_it_today: Find something to genuinely praise someone else for today — especially if you feel a little jealous of them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Ten disciples in a cluster looking disgruntled and cross-armed at James and John who stand slightly apart, a tense atmosphere between the two groups, Jesus watching from a slight distance. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus called them all together and said: 'You know how the rulers of the world work — they use their power to control people, and the important ones love to boss others around.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'The rulers of the world love to control people and show off their power.'
verses.meaning: Jesus described the world's model of greatness: power over people, authority used for self. He was about to show them a completely different way.
verses.memory_phrase: The world's way and God's way are opposites.
verses.prayer: God, help me not want power over people but to use any influence I have to serve and bless others.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of leaders who use their power to control others? What does that feel like?
verses.family_discussion: The world teaches that greatness means having power over others. What do you think godly leadership looks like in a family? At school? In a country?
verses.do_it_today: If you have any responsibility or leadership at home or school, look for one way to use it to serve someone instead of control them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gathering all twelve disciples around Him in a circle, gesturing outward with one hand to indicate the wider world, His expression contrasting with an imagined scene of proud rulers in the distance. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But among you it must be different! If anyone wants to be great in My kingdom, they must become the servant of everyone else.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'In My kingdom, greatness means being a servant to everyone else.'
verses.meaning: This was a complete reversal. Jesus did not say ambition was wrong — He said the direction of ambition must change: from being served to serving. True greatness serves.
verses.memory_phrase: Greatness means serving.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me want to serve people rather than be served. Change my heart to be like Yours.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of someone who is truly great because they serve others? What do they do?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the greatest person is the servant. Who serves most in your family? How can everyone share in serving more?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one person at home, school, or church and do something to serve them without being asked.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus spreading His hands wide and speaking with warm authority to His disciples gathered in a close circle, their faces shifting from confusion to understanding, afternoon light behind. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'And if you want to be the most important person of all, you must be the servant of absolutely everyone.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'The most important person is the one who serves absolutely everyone.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was not being poetic — He was being completely literal. The pathway to true greatness goes downward, not upward. The greatest leaders in God's kingdom are the ones who stoop the lowest to serve.
verses.memory_phrase: Serve all — that is true greatness.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that wants to serve everyone — not just people who can help me back.
verses.discussion_question: What would a school look like if the most popular kids were also the most helpful and kind?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the chiefest would be the servant of ALL — not some. Who are the people in your community that nobody serves? How might your family serve them?
verses.do_it_today: Serve someone who cannot give you anything back — an elderly neighbour, a younger child, someone often overlooked.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child in humble clothing cheerfully carrying water and food to a long line of different people — young and old, rich and poor — while a crown of soft golden light glows above the child's head. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The Son of Man — Me — did not come so that people would serve Me. I came to serve, and to give My life as a ransom — a rescue payment — to set many people free.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He came to serve and to give His life to set us free. He is the greatest servant of all.
verses.meaning: This is the mission statement of Jesus in one sentence. He came to serve, not to be served — and ultimately, He gave His very life as a ransom to free us from sin. He is the ultimate example of servant greatness.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to serve and to save us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for serving me and giving Your life for me. Help me live in a way that honours what You did.
verses.discussion_question: What does 'ransom' mean? How did Jesus pay a ransom for us?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave His life as a ransom — a price paid to set us free. Talk about what it means that Jesus set your family free. What were you freed from? What were you freed for?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw the words 'Jesus set me free' and put them somewhere you will see them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall with arms wide open, a warm light radiating around Him, a diverse crowd of people walking free from darkness into light behind Him, the sense of something enormous and beautiful just accomplished. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, sat by the highway side begging.
verses.little_bible: They arrived at the city of Jericho. As Jesus and the crowd were leaving the city, a blind man named Bartimaeus — the son of Timaeus — sat beside the road, begging for money.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Near the city of Jericho, a blind man called Bartimaeus sat begging by the road.
verses.meaning: Bartimaeus had nothing — no sight, no income, no status — just a spot on the side of the road. But he was about to have the most important moment of his life. God sees the ones the world walks past.
verses.memory_phrase: God sees the ones the world walks past.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You see me even when I feel invisible. Help me also see the people others walk past.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever walked past someone who needed help? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Bartimaeus was at the very edge of the road — forgotten by most people. Who are the 'Bartimaeuses' in your neighbourhood or community? How could your family reach out to them?
verses.do_it_today: Look around today for someone on the edge who is being overlooked. Do one thing to see them and make them feel noticed.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dusty road outside a walled city, a blind man in a worn cloak sitting on the ground at the roadside, a cup in front of him, a flowing river of people passing by without looking, warm dry light. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.little_bible: When Bartimaeus heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth passing by, he started calling out at the top of his voice: 'Jesus! Son of David! Please have mercy on me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Bartimaeus heard Jesus was passing. He shouted loudly: 'Jesus! Son of David! Have mercy on me!'
verses.meaning: Bartimaeus called Jesus 'Son of David' — a title for the Messiah. He knew who Jesus was, even though he could not see Him. Real faith recognises Jesus even in the dark.
verses.memory_phrase: Call out to Jesus — He hears you!
verses.prayer: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me! I call out to You right now. Please hear my prayer.
verses.discussion_question: Bartimaeus was blind but he 'saw' who Jesus really was. What did he call Him? What does that mean?
verses.family_discussion: Bartimaeus recognised Jesus as the Messiah. Does your family know who Jesus really is? Talk about what the title 'Son of David' meant — Jesus is the promised King!
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud or write: 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!' — use it as your prayer today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A blind beggar on the ground suddenly alert and leaning forward with both hands cupped around his mouth, calling out urgently, a look of desperate hope on his face, a crowd of people and dusty road around him. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.little_bible: Many people in the crowd told him to be quiet and stop making noise. But instead of going quiet, Bartimaeus cried out even LOUDER: 'Son of David! Have mercy on me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The crowd told him to be quiet. But Bartimaeus shouted even louder: 'Son of David! Mercy!'
verses.meaning: Bartimaeus refused to let the crowd silence him. When people tell you to stop praying, stop hoping, stop calling out to Jesus — that is exactly the wrong time to stop. He is the model of persistent faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't stop calling out to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me keep calling out to You even when I feel like giving up. You are always listening.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when people tell you that praying doesn't work or that God doesn't care? What can we learn from Bartimaeus?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd tried to silence Bartimaeus. What 'crowds' — pressures, doubts, busy-ness — try to silence your family's prayers? How can you shout louder together?
verses.do_it_today: Pray about something you have been putting off or been discouraged about. Pray louder and longer than usual today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Bartimaeus standing now, arms outstretched toward the distant figure of Jesus, head thrown back, mouth wide open calling out, while people in the crowd frown and gesture for him to sit down, he ignores them completely. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
verses.little_bible: Jesus stopped walking. He told the crowd to bring the blind man to Him. So the crowd changed their tune: 'Cheer up! Get up! Jesus is calling for you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus stopped and said: 'Bring him to Me!' The crowd said: 'Cheer up! Jesus is calling you!'
verses.meaning: Jesus stood still for one crying blind beggar. The whole crowd — who had been ignoring and silencing Bartimaeus — now became his helpers. When Jesus calls you, even the crowd changes its mind.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus stops for you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You stop for me. Even when I feel small and overlooked, You hear me and You come.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel knowing that Jesus stopped everything He was doing to respond to one blind beggar?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus stopped the whole procession for Bartimaeus. Talk about a time God answered a prayer in your family that felt too small or too unlikely. How did He stop and respond?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'Jesus hears you. He has not forgotten you. He is calling your name.' Say it to yourself too.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus stopped in the middle of a crowd, hand raised to halt everyone, turning back with a warm smile to look toward where Bartimaeus is, the crowd parting and some now pointing toward the blind man to beckon him. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.
verses.little_bible: Bartimaeus threw off his cloak — the cloak he used to collect coins — jumped to his feet, and made his way to Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Bartimaeus threw off his cloak, jumped up, and came to Jesus.
verses.meaning: He threw away his begging cloak — the symbol of his whole way of surviving — because when Jesus called, everything else became unimportant. He left behind his old life to run to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Leave your old life behind and run to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me throw off anything that holds me back from coming to You. I want to run to You with nothing in the way.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the cloak represented to Bartimaeus? Why was throwing it away such a big deal?
verses.family_discussion: Bartimaeus dropped his cloak — his security — to run to Jesus. What are the 'cloaks' your family might need to leave behind to come closer to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Write on a piece of paper one thing that holds you back from Jesus. Then rip it up and pray: 'Jesus, I choose You instead.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Bartimaeus mid-leap, his worn cloak falling behind him on the ground, arms reaching forward, face full of eager joy, stumbling along the path toward Jesus who waits with open arms in the distance. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do for thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked him: 'What do you want Me to do for you?' The blind man said: 'Lord — Teacher — I want to see again!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'What do you want?' Bartimaeus said: 'Lord, I want to see!'
verses.meaning: Jesus asked the same question He had asked James and John — 'What do you want?' But Bartimaeus' answer was completely different. He didn't ask for power or position. He asked for the one thing he truly needed: sight.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask Jesus for what you really need.
verses.prayer: Lord, I want to see — really see You, see Your love, see what You are doing in my life. Open my eyes!
verses.discussion_question: Bartimaeus asked for what he really needed. If you could ask Jesus for anything, what would it be?
verses.family_discussion: Bartimaeus asked for sight — the thing he actually needed, not just wanted. Talk as a family about what you truly need from God right now. Pray for those things together.
verses.do_it_today: Ask God for what you really need today — not just what you want, but what truly matters.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus crouching down to the level of Bartimaeus who kneels before Him, face upturned with total trust, Jesus' hands cupped gently near the man's face, a halo of warm light between them. Watercolour storybook.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Go — your faith has healed you!' Immediately Bartimaeus could see — and he followed Jesus down the road.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Your faith made you well!' Immediately Bartimaeus could see — and he followed Jesus.
verses.meaning: Bartimaeus was healed instantly and completely. He didn't go home — he followed Jesus. That is the perfect end to this story: not just sight restored, but a life turned toward Jesus. Persistent faith is always rewarded.
verses.memory_phrase: Faith in Jesus always changes everything.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for healing and for calling us to follow You. I want to follow You all the days of my life.
verses.discussion_question: What did Bartimaeus do right after he was healed? Why did he follow Jesus instead of just going home?
verses.family_discussion: Bartimaeus followed Jesus the moment he could see. What does it look like for your family to follow Jesus — not just to receive things from Him, but to actually go where He goes?
verses.do_it_today: Write this down: 'Jesus healed me and I will follow Him.' Put it somewhere you will see tomorrow morning.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in a dusty cloak standing on a road, eyes wide open in wonder, looking at Jesus who is nearby, a crowd around them, bright light and blue sky above, the man's face full of tears and joy and amazement, already taking his first step to follow. Watercolour storybook.
